purpose: Defines the worker agent deviation reporting protocol used in !p_track swarms. Specifies what counts as a deviation (anything done that was not explicitly specified in the dispatch prompt), the three-tier severity classification (CRITICAL/MODERATE/MINOR) used by the master to decide whether to replan, the exact JSON entry shape for the deviations[] array, the dual-write requirement (deviations[] AND a corresponding logs[] entry at level 'deviation' in the same write), and how the master uses each severity tier. Also covers the ambiguity-resolution protocol where unclear requirements must produce a documented MODERATE deviation rather than a silent guess.
gotchas: Deviations must be written IMMEDIATELY when they occur — never batched for end-of-task. The dashboard yellow badge appears in real-time from this write.
A deviation REQUIRES two simultaneous updates in the same progress file write: an entry in deviations[] AND a logs[] entry at level 'deviation'. Skipping either is a failure.
CRITICAL severity may trigger replanning of downstream tasks — misclassifying a function-signature change as MODERATE will cause downstream workers to fail against stale interfaces.
Silent guesses at ambiguous requirements look like bugs to the master; documented conservative choices look like good judgment. Never resolve ambiguity without a deviation entry.
Modifying any file not in the FILES list is a MODERATE deviation by default — workers commonly forget that scope expansion is itself reportable.
Timestamps must be captured live via `date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"` — never constructed from memory.
patterns: Severity-tagged deviations: every deviation entry MUST include a severity field (CRITICAL/MODERATE/MINOR), not optional.
Dual-write protocol: deviations[] entry + logs[] entry at level 'deviation' written together atomically.
Ambiguity resolution: choose most conservative option (changes least, breaks nothing, follows existing patterns), document as MODERATE deviation, log at warn level.

Severity decision rule: any change affecting an interface/API/contract downstream tasks depend on => CRITICAL; same outcome via different means => MODERATE; cosmetic/naming with no functional impact => MINOR.
conventions: Deviation entry shape: { at: ISO8601, severity: 'CRITICAL'|'MODERATE'|'MINOR', description: string }
Use em-dash separator in descriptions: 'What changed — why' (matches example pattern through file).
Mandatory write #4 in the progress protocol corresponds to deviation reporting.
